generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance GantryBridgeMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator controlling the horizontal movement of the gantry bridge, interfacing with position encoders and receiving motor commands from the controller."
]

instance TrolleyMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator controlling the lateral movement of the trolley along the gantry bridge, receiving velocity and position commands from the embedded controller."
]

instance HoistMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator controlling the vertical movement of the hook and puck, interfacing with load sensors and receiving torque commands from the controller."
]

instance CraneController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded controller interfacing with all actuators (gantry, trolley, hoist motors) and sensors, executing closed-loop control algorithms for trajectory following."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance GantryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Industrial encoder measuring the absolute position of the gantry bridge with high precision, transmitting data via serial/TCP/IP."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GantryPositionData
]

instance TrolleyPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Industrial encoder measuring the absolute position of the trolley along the gantry bridge, transmitting data via serial/TCP/IP."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TrolleyPositionData
]

instance HookPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Industrial encoder measuring the vertical position of the hook and puck, transmitting data via serial/TCP/IP."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HookPositionData
]

instance GantryVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Velocity sensor (e.g., tachometer) measuring the linear velocity of the gantry bridge, transmitting data via serial/TCP/IP."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GantryVelocityData
]

instance TrolleyVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Velocity sensor measuring the linear velocity of the trolley, transmitting data via serial/TCP/IP."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TrolleyVelocityData
]

instance PuckLoadCell : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Load cell measuring the weight and swinging motion of the puck, transmitting data via serial/TCP/IP."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PuckLoadData
]

instance ControllerFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor providing feedback on executed motor commands and actuator states, transmitting data via serial/TCP/IP."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ControllerFeedbackData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance GantryPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time Cartesian coordinates of the gantry bridge transmitted at high frequency (10-100 Hz) via serial/TCP/IP or MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GantryPositionEncoder
]

instance TrolleyPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time Cartesian coordinates of the trolley transmitted at high frequency (10-100 Hz) via serial/TCP/IP or MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TrolleyPositionEncoder
]

instance HookPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time vertical position of the hook and puck transmitted at high frequency (10-100 Hz) via serial/TCP/IP or MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HookPositionEncoder
]

instance GantryVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time velocity measurements of the gantry bridge transmitted at high frequency (10-100 Hz) via serial/TCP/IP or MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GantryVelocitySensor
]

instance TrolleyVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time velocity measurements of the trolley transmitted at high frequency (10-100 Hz) via serial/TCP/IP or MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TrolleyVelocitySensor
]

instance PuckLoadData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time load and swinging motion data of the puck transmitted at high frequency (10-100 Hz) via serial/TCP/IP or MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PuckLoadCell
]

instance ControllerFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time controller signals and actuator feedback transmitted at high frequency (10-100 Hz) via serial/TCP/IP or MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ControllerFeedbackSensor
]

instance PhysicalCraneInterfaceData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Aggregated sensor and actuator data from the physical crane, preprocessed and transmitted via MQTT to the digital twin components."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mathematical model of the crane's kinematics using Denavit-Hartenberg parameters, implemented in MATLAB/Simulink or Python with PyRobot."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KinematicSimulationEnabler, TrajectoryGeneratorEnabler
]

instance DynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Rigid-body dynamics model for physics simulation using Bullet Physics or ODE in Gazebo, enabling real-time validation of trajectories."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PhysicsSimulationEnabler
]

instance ControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Optimal control model for trajectory planning and adaptive control, implemented using Pyomo or CasADi in Python."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryGeneratorEnabler
]

instance RawSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed time-series data from all sensors, stored in TimescaleDB for historical analysis and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GantryPositionData, TrolleyPositionData, HookPositionData, GantryVelocityData, TrolleyVelocityData, PuckLoadData, ControllerFeedbackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LoggingEnabler, PreprocessingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Filtered and normalized sensor data (e.g., Kalman-filtered swinging motion, Cartesian coordinates) synchronized for trajectory alignment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PhysicalCraneInterfaceData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessingEnabler
]

instance TrajectoryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Generated and executed trajectories (position, velocity, acceleration profiles) for the gantry, trolley, and hoist."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryGeneratorEnabler
]

instance SimulationResults : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output from physics and control simulations, including predicted trajectories and system responses."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PhysicalCraneInterfaceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python program acting as a bridge between physical hardware and digital twin, handling data aggregation and protocol conversion (serial/TCP/IP to MQTT)."
]

instance MQTTBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight message broker routing sensor data and control signals between physical crane and digital twin services (e.g., Trajectory Generator, Simulation Service)."
]

instance TimescaleDBEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing raw and processed sensor data, enabling post-hoc validation and anomaly detection."
]

instance KinematicSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine (e.g., MATLAB/Simulink, PyRobot) executing kinematic models for trajectory prediction."
]

instance PhysicsSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics simulation engine (e.g., Bullet Physics, Gazebo) for rigid-body dynamics validation."
]

instance TrajectoryGeneratorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Service generating optimal trajectories using control models (e.g., Pyomo, CasADi) for energy-efficient motion planning."
]

instance PreprocessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data preprocessing pipeline applying Kalman filtering, normalization, and timestamp synchronization for sensor data."
]

instance LoggingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Application logging raw and processed data to TimescaleDB for historical analysis and trend detection."
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization framework (e.g., ROS 2 RViz, Unity) displaying CAD models synchronized with real-time sensor data."
]

// C6: Services
instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates optimal trajectories for the gantry, trolley, and hoist based on kinematic and dynamic constraints."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryInsight
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Compares executed trajectories with simulated predictions in near real-time to detect discrepancies and validate model fidelity."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelPlantSynchronizationInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implements real-time adaptive control policies to refine actuator commands dynamically for energy efficiency and collision avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptiveControlInsight, EnergyEfficiencyInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Monitors sensor data and control signals for abnormal patterns (e.g., encoder failures, load imbalances) using machine learning and rule-based thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDiagnosisInsight, PredictiveMaintenanceInsight
]

instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Logs sensor data and actuator commands for post-mortem analysis, trend detection, and performance benchmarking."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalAnalyticsInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time and historical visualization of crane state (position, velocity, load) synchronized with CAD models."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight, EducationalDemoInsight
]

instance SystemEvolutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Tracks and documents changes in the digital twin using DarTwin notation for model updates, service additions, and configuration changes."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemVersioningInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OptimalTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Generated trajectories optimized for energy efficiency and collision avoidance, validated against kinematic and dynamic constraints."
]

instance ModelPlantSynchronizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time comparison between digital twin predictions and physical measurements to ensure model fidelity."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified discrepancies between simulated and executed trajectories, indicating potential sensor faults or model drift."
]

instance AdaptiveControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustments to actuator commands based on real-time sensor feedback and control model predictions."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized control policies reducing energy consumption while maintaining trajectory accuracy."
]

instance FaultDiagnosisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Classified faults (e.g., encoder failure, load imbalance) based on abnormal patterns in sensor data."
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted component failures (e.g., motor wear, sensor degradation) to enable proactive maintenance."
]

instance OperationalAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Trends and performance metrics derived from historical sensor and actuator data for benchmarking and improvement."
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Live visualization of crane state (position, velocity, load) synchronized with 3D CAD models."
]

instance EducationalDemoInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive demonstrations of digital twin capabilities for educational purposes."
]

instance SystemVersioningInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Documented changes in the digital twin's models, services, and configurations using DarTwin notation."
]

instance RecalibrateControllerAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically triggers recalibration of the physical controller when discrepancies exceed predefined thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TriggerAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generates alerts for maintenance teams when faults are detected in sensor or actuator data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance UpdateTrajectoryAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamically adjusts the trajectory plan based on real-time sensor feedback and control model predictions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LogDataAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Stores raw and processed sensor data in TimescaleDB for historical analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance VisualizeStateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Updates the 3D visualization dashboard with current crane state data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DocumentChangeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Records changes in the digital twin's models and services using DarTwin notation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this Digital Twin system is mediated by the **Control Application** and **Physical Crane Interface**, with the following technical specifics:
  - **Command Types**: Generated by the **Trajectory Generator Service**, which produces **optimal movement trajectories** (e.g., smooth, jerk-limited paths) to minimize residual swinging motion of the puck. Commands include:
    - **Position/Velocity Setpoints**: Cartesian or joint-space trajectories for the gantry, trolley, and hook.
    - **Controller Parameters**: PID gains or adaptive control signals for the crane’s motors.
  - **Control Message Formats**: Likely **structured JSON or binary protocols** (e.g., ROS messages or custom TCP/IP payloads) transmitted via the **Physical Crane Interface** to the crane’s controllers.
  - **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**: Not explicitly stated, but inferred to include:
    - **Trajectory Validation**: The **Continuous Validation Service** compares executed trajectories (physical) with simulated ones (digital) using **distance metrics** (e.g., Euclidean error in position/velocity). If thresholds (e.g., ±5% deviation) are breached, alerts are triggered via the **Dashboard**.
    - **Command Integrity**: Implicitly ensured by the **Control Application**’s reliance on validated trajectories from the **Trajectory Generator Service**.
  - **Feedback Systems**: Closed-loop control is implied, with the **Physical Crane Interface** relaying real-time sensor data (e.g., actual position/velocity) back to the **Control Application** for adaptive adjustments.
  - **Conflict Resolution**: Not explicitly detailed, but the **MQTT Broker** may prioritize messages (e.g., emergency stops override trajectory commands) or implement **last-write-wins** semantics for concurrent updates.
  - **Command Execution Confirmation**: Likely achieved via **acknowledgment messages** (e.g., MQTT QoS 1/2) or **state synchronization** between the **Control Application** and **Physical Crane Interface**, ensuring commands are executed as intended."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation in the provided exemplars follows an **ad-hoc, lightweight orchestration pattern** without reliance on commercial frameworks. GreenhouseDT and Air Quality Management exemplify **loosely coupled architectures** using **containerization/virtualization** (e.g., virtual machines with pre-recorded data in GreenhouseDT) and **message brokers (ActiveMQ)** for inter-component communication. Resource management is decentralized, with **Raspberry Pi-based sensor nodes** handling localized data collection and processing. The Air Quality Management exemplar further integrates **AutomationML-based digital twins** and **Microsoft Azure tools**, indicating a hybrid cloud-edge orchestration. The lab-scale gantry crane’s C4-based architecture suggests a **context-aware, containerized deployment**, though no explicit coordination protocols (e.g., consensus, leader election) are described. The overall pattern emphasizes **domain-specific adaptability** over standardized frameworks."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar emphasizes **self-adaptive behavioral models**, implying dynamic fidelity adjustments to align the digital twin with the physical system’s evolving state. Validation is inferred through **prerecorded data** in virtual machines, enabling offline testing against historical or simulated scenarios. The **Air Quality Management** system prioritizes **model-driven engineering** with **AutomationML**, suggesting structured validation via **runtime visualization** and **prediction accuracy metrics**, though no explicit uncertainty quantification or error correction mechanisms are described. For the **gantry crane**, the ad-hoc approach lacks documented fidelity requirements or validation methods, relying instead on **containerized deployment** for reproducibility. Quality assurance processes are not explicitly detailed, but the use of **commercial cloud tools (Azure)** and **standardized protocols (AutomationML)** in the Air Quality exemplar implies adherence to industry best practices for data integrity and model traceability."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin’s Technical Connection** infrastructure is designed for low-latency, high-reliability communication between components. Key details include:
  - **Communication Protocols**:
    - **MQTT** (via the **MQTT Broker**) for lightweight, event-driven signaling between services (e.g., **Trajectory Generator** → **Control Application**).
    - **TCP/IP** or **serial protocols** for direct hardware-software communication (e.g., **Physical Crane Interface** ↔ crane controllers).
    - **TimescaleDB’s internal protocols** (e.g., PostgreSQL-based) for structured data storage/retrieval.
  - **Network Topology and Architecture**:
    - **Star topology** centered around the **MQTT Broker**, with services (e.g., **Dashboard**, **Simulation Service**) subscribing/publishing to topics.
    - **Local-area network (LAN)** for on-premise deployment, given the lab-scale scope.
  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
    - **Low-latency (<100 ms)** for real-time trajectory validation (e.g., **Continuous Validation Service**).
    - **Moderate bandwidth** (~1–10 Mbps) to handle time-series data (e.g., sensor streams, trajectory logs) in **TimescaleDB**.
  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
    - **MQTT QoS levels** (e.g., QoS 1 for at-least-once delivery of critical commands).
    - **Database replication** in **TimescaleDB** (not explicitly stated but inferred for fault tolerance).
    - **Fallback to manual operation** if digital twin services fail (as the physical crane can operate independently).
  - **Security Protocols**:
    - Not explicitly detailed, but likely includes:
      - **Authentication**: Service-level credentials for MQTT topics (e.g., username/password or TLS client certificates).
      - **Encryption**: TLS for MQTT/TCP communication if deployed in a shared network.
      - **Access Control**: Role-based permissions (e.g., **Operator** vs. **Researcher** access to the **Dashboard**)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin’s Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is tailored for a **lab-scale, on-premise** environment with the following specifics:
  - **Hosting Platform**: **On-premise** (local server or workstation), given the system’s small scale and lack of cloud references. The **TimescaleDB** database and **MQTT Broker** are likely co-located with the physical crane for minimal latency.
  - **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
    - **CPU/GPU**: Moderate (e.g., multi-core x86 or embedded GPU) to handle real-time trajectory generation and simulation.
    - **RAM**: ~4–16 GB for running Python services (e.g., **Trajectory Generator**, **Simulation Service**) concurrently.
    - **Storage**: **TimescaleDB** (PostgreSQL-based) requires SSD storage for time-series data, with capacity scaling based on logging frequency (e.g., 100 Hz sensor data → ~10 GB/month).
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**:
    - **Vertical scaling** (e.g., upgrading the host machine) for increased data throughput or simulation complexity.
    - **Modular service design** (e.g., **Trajectory Generator** as a standalone container) allows for future horizontal scaling if deployed in a distributed environment.
  - **Deployment Models Supported**:
    - **Single-node deployment** (all services on one machine) for simplicity.
    - **Containerized deployment** (implied by the C4 model’s container abstraction) using tools like Docker (though not explicitly mentioned).
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
    - **High availability** achieved via:
      - **Redundant MQTT Broker** (not stated but inferred for critical systems).
      - **Periodic backups** of **TimescaleDB** data.
      - **Graceful degradation** (e.g., switching to manual control if the **Dashboard** fails)."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration in the Digital Twin exemplars is **limited to domain-specific, lightweight interoperability** without standardized enterprise integration patterns. GreenhouseDT relies on **InfluxDB (time-series database)** and **ActiveMQ (message broker)** for internal data exchange, while Air Quality Management uses **AutomationML** for digital twin modeling and **Microsoft Azure tools** for cloud-based pipeline execution. Both exemplars lack explicit mention of **external system dependencies** (e.g., ERP, MES) or **cross-Digital Twin interoperability standards** (e.g., OPC UA, FIWARE). The lab-scale gantry crane’s architecture (C4 Model) implies **modular integration** but provides no details on protocols or external system linkages. The ad-hoc approach prioritizes **self-contained deployment** over broad horizontal scalability."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin exemplars adhere to the following **standards and interoperability frameworks**:
  - **AutomationML**: Used in the **Air Quality Management exemplar** for structured digital twin modeling, ensuring compatibility with industrial automation standards.
  - **Industry-Specific Domains**:
    - **Agricultural**: GreenhouseDT aligns with low-cost IoT/agricultural monitoring standards (e.g., sensor data formats, time-series databases like **InfluxDB**).
    - **Construction/Indoor Air Quality**: The Air Quality Management exemplar targets **virus spread prevention**, implying compliance with **ASHRAE 62.1** (ventilation standards) or similar health/safety regulations, though not explicitly stated.
    - **Logistics/Cyber-Physical Systems**: The gantry crane exemplar (logistics domain) lacks explicit standardization but uses **C4 Model** for architecture visualization, a de facto standard for software/system modeling.
  - **Cloud/Interoperability**:
    - **Microsoft Azure** tools (Air Quality Management) suggest alignment with **Microsoft’s interoperability standards** (e.g., REST APIs, Azure IoT Hub).
    - **Message Brokers**: GreenhouseDT uses **ActiveMQ**, a standard for **JMS/AMQP-based messaging**, ensuring cross-platform communication.
  - **Certification**: No explicit certification requirements (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 62443) are mentioned. The exemplars prioritize **ad-hoc reproducibility** over formal certification."
]
